steps.title: Identify the contrast between appearance and reality
steps.content: The first sentence describes the 1920 method as seeming rudimentary. The second sentence then reveals that modern technology confirmed it was relatively accurate. The transition needs to signal this shift from initial impression to what the evidence actually shows.

steps.title: Match the transition to the contrast
steps.content: That said, is a concession transition meaning despite that impression, which bridges the apparent rudimentariness to the evidence proving accuracy. The other three choices either introduce an example, signal a consequence, or restate the same idea—none express this contrast. That matches choice B — B is correct.
